hypothesis: If an LLM-based materials design agent is augmented with external tools (short- and long-term memory, periodic table, and a knowledge base) and run in an iterative loop with a diffusion crystal-structure generator and a formation-energy predictor, then it will achieve a higher success rate of meeting specified formation-energy targets (within ±0.25 eV/atom) and better validity–uniqueness–novelty metrics than tool-free LLMs or a standalone diffusion baseline because tool-informed reasoning and feedback guide chemically plausible, diverse, and targeted proposals.
